mnemonics: QR = Quality Rags (Q is for orthonormal quality, R is for upper triangular rags)
Gram-Schmidt into QR (The process is the key to the factorization)
keyPoints: Any matrix A with linearly independent columns can be decomposed into A = QR.
Q is an m x n matrix with orthonormal columns that form a basis for the column space of A.
R is an n x n upper triangular invertible matrix.
commonMistakes: Attempting QR-factorization on a matrix with linearly dependent columns.
Forgetting that the columns of Q must be orthonormal, not just orthogonal.
Mistaking R for a lower triangular matrix instead of an upper triangular one.
